J.B. here, time for your daily motivation slash skills training. Great story here. I want to talk about the power of unconscious communication, but really the form of body language. I was in Mongolia, we should not, right? A few years back. Great place, only just full of Mongolians. I love Mongolians, right? They're nice people, right? You know, they all think they're related to Genghis Khan, and I guess they probably are, which is a scary thought in terms of inbreeding. Anyway, so you know, everyone there is very nice, very affable, and I met, you know, a couple thousand people at my event there, and there was this sort of massive meet and greet beforehand, and no one really stuck out, grabbed my attention. Man, woman, they all looked pretty much the same. They're about 30 minutes into this, you know, meet and greet, some guy caught my eye, out of like, you know, 100 yards out. I took one, like, whoa. I'm like, that is the most important man in the room. I bet he's the wealthiest man too. Guess what? Turned out he was then the major senator, a politician, the country's most famous athlete. Now he's their president. How do I know that? Well, it wasn't that his suit was so much more expensive. He had a nice suit. It was the way it fit. It was the way he stood. It was the way he held his head. It was just everything, it was just the way he was shaking people's hands. There was a certain way this guy had about him that just commanded respect, and you knew the moment you laid eyes on him, that this was a person not only worth listening to, but someone that can help you get done. A person of power, an authority figure. Unconscious communication is more powerful than it's conscious, counter-wise, much more powerful than it was, and it hits you right in the gut. Just remember this. We move through the world, and we can't possibly take in all of the sensory data that's being thrown at us every single minute. In fact, our conscious mind with its relatively measly processing power could only take in 4% of the sensory data that comes at us. 96% gets deleted out. It just has to, it can't deal. So what happens is, remember, 4% is what your conscious awareness is. Right now, you're focused on me. My voice, my words, looking at me, trying to pick up my body gestures, cues. Maybe there's a loud noise around you somewhere, or something that you can't block out. That's it. 4%. Everything else, the pressure of your seat on your ass or your legs, the temperature of the wind on your skin, little clink clang noise that might have bothered you only a minute ago. You've been tuned out, habituated out as the phrase goes, and your brain only goes after that 4% that it deems to be important. The key to remember here is that the other 96% is not lost. That is all stored away almost in a blueprint fashion in your unconscious mind, which has unlimited processing power. You get it? Unlimited. It's like the hard drive. It's like the Amazon, like your cloud server. Imagine that. It's like unlimited storage capacity. While your random access memory, you know that what's on your computer, it gets spitting real death. Right? Well, the point is, is what happens is because of the limited processing power in the conscious mind, everything gets deleted out and then generalized by your unconscious mind, which as it's doing that, it's also beating your heart, keeping your vessels dilated, doing every your digestion, everything. It's doing everything and it's making a perfect blueprinted recollection of everything that you've ever seen, heard, touched, felt, or went through and experienced in your life. It generalizes that and then lays down these patterns of behavior in your unconscious mind. It's those patterns of behavior, my friend. Those patterns are what dictates the gut reactions that we have when we see someone, when we lay eyes on them for the first time, we hear their voice on the phone. It's those patterns that we are looking to hit. So if we have been conditioned since we're yay big to say, this is how sharp people dress, this is how they stand, this is how winners carry themselves, this is how they talk, this is how they don't talk. That's been conditioned into us, laid down indelibly in our unconscious mind, starting from our very, very early years. We could start twice when you start to talk. So you really have that laid down, right? Two or three years earlier for some of us. And then what happens is once someone lays eyes on you, it's an instant boom, quarter of a second, bam to the temporal lobe back. That's where your brain sees things, your temporal lobe. And a quarter of a second later to the prefrontal cortex where the judgment center of the brain is located and that's where the decision gets made. I ain't making you up, okay? You know what? I'm not. A, I was a bio, a biology and chemistry major, right? But I also studied this stuff. It's just true. You can Google it, right? It happens up here. You can use this to such massive advantage in sales because what happens is if you just want to tip the odds dramatically in your favor, dress a certain way, talk a certain way, carry yourself a certain way, how is that way? The way experts do. The way experts do. Don't you want to like, I don't, I don't know. Find out what, I don't care what field you're in, okay? Matter what field you're in, it has a specific way that experts in that field dress it act and it's different in every industry. The top tennis professional teacher in the world, a guy that that in Federer would hire, right? He's not dressing like a Wall Street banker, but he's drawing like just like a slob. You know, guys, every industry has their best way of dressing. It's called the law of congruency. You want to dress, act, carry yourself, talk, you know, it's congruent. You know, it's expected in your industry. Do this. And I promise you, you set yourself up for this massive downhill ride. Everything gets very easy. Please don't forget this today, tomorrow, all week long and after that, your conscious mind is meagerly equipped to deal with this stuff. It's your unconscious mind that's all powerful here. Remember that and act accordingly. It's the greatest tip I can give you here.
